behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: loader combines file config + `TOOL_` env overrides, validates required keys, and reports missing key path exactly. Hypothetical elsewhere: CLI exit code wiring, terminal formatting, OS-specific env quirks. MOCK-MISS: yes, failure is at module handoff (merge + validation + error formatting), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process env/file fixtures are enough. MOCK-HIDE: mocking either file or env parser could hide precedence/absence bugs, so test real module wiring. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required for this assertion; (b) no AI/ML output dependency; (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text only requires correct missing-path reporting from loader inputs, not real subprocess/HTTP/OS-only effects.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: run config loader with a test YAML missing `database.host` and no matching `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST` in env; assert returned/raised actionable error includes exact `database.host` path.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: loader reads env var and uses it in path resolution for config file location. Hypothetical elsewhere: directory creation permissions, shell expansion edge cases across platforms. MOCK-MISS: yes, bug can occur where startup loader ignores resolver output even if resolver unit passes. REAL-INFRA: no strict need for subprocess or external network; temp paths and patched env in-process are sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: mocking path resolution too aggressively can hide wiring bug. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS primitive beyond normal local path handling; (b) no AI/ML dependency; (c) no multi-agent sequence. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text is path-selection logic (`XDG_CONFIG_HOME` vs `~/.config`), not OS-event-dependent behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory, invoke loader startup path resolution, and assert attempted config path is `<XDG_CONFIG_HOME>/tool/config.yaml` (or tool-specific relative path), not under home `.config`.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: conflict resolution between parsed YAML values and parsed `TOOL_` env overrides. Hypothetical elsewhere: secrets manager integration, remote config backends. MOCK-MISS: yes, precedence bugs are at module boundary (file loader + env mapper + merge policy). REAL-INFRA: no real subprocess/network needed. MOCK-HIDE: a stub merge or stub env parser could always pass and miss true precedence behavior. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess; (b) no AI/ML output; (c) no chained agentic steps. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text only asserts precedence rule, which is reproducible with in-process fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: provide YAML with key `k=v_file` and env `TOOL_K=v_env`; run loader and assert effective config value is `v_env`.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: real YAML parse failure is surfaced and transformed into actionable message with parser-reported line number. Hypothetical elsewhere: localization of messages, shell coloring. MOCK-MISS: yes, a fake parser exception may not match real YAML library exception shape/line metadata. REAL-INFRA: no subprocess/network/external API required. MOCK-HIDE: yes, stubbing parser can hide mismatch in exception fields and line extraction. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required OS/network/subprocess; (b) no AI/ML output dependency; (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text requires parser error propagation details, not OS-level behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: load malformed YAML from fixture (known bad line), assert loader fails with message containing the expected line number from the real YAML parser.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: startup flow checks directory existence, creates directory, writes default config file, then continues load. Hypothetical elsewhere: cross-filesystem atomicity, concurrent process races, OS-specific file watchers. MOCK-MISS: yes, pure mocks can miss real path/write sequencing issues. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem interaction is required, but this fits Integration (local filesystem) without needing subprocess/external services. MOCK-HIDE: yes, mocked FS may always succeed and hide path/join/order bugs. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no explicit need for subprocess/network/real external API; (b) no AI/ML dependency; (c) no agent pipeline. Rejection gate: considered System, rejected because behavior text does not require OS-specific event semantics or subprocess behavior, only local create-and-write startup flow.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with temp home/config root: ensure target config directory is absent, run loader startup, assert directory now exists, defaults file is written at expected path, and loader proceeds to return usable config.
